sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed after clicking (35, 19)
print("Current magenta dots:")
for n in current_frame.segmentation['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'M' and n['pixels'] == 4:
        center = (sum(p[0] for p in n['boundary'])/len(n['boundary']),
                  sum(p[1] for p in n['boundary'])/len(n['boundary']))
        if center[0] > 5:
            print(f"  M block: center~({center[0]:.0f},{center[1]:.0f}), boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Check blue blob
for n in current_frame.segmentation['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'b':
        center = (sum(p[0] for p in n['boundary'])/len(n['boundary']),
                  sum(p[1] for p in n['boundary'])/len(n['boundary']))
        print(f"\nBlue blob: pixels={n['pixels']}, center~({center[0]:.0f},{center[1]:.0f})")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The magenta block at (36, 18) moved to (34, 18)! It moved up by 2 rows. So clicking on a position adjacent to a magenta block moves the block toward that position. The block moved from center (36, 18) to (34, 18), which is 2 rows up. I clicked at (35, 19), which is up-right from the block.
So the mechanic is: clicking on an empty space near a magenta block moves that block toward the clicked position. The block seems to move diagonally or in the direction of the click.
The goal is probably to move all magenta blocks to the blue blob (or to specific target positions). Let me figure out the movement pattern more precisely.
The block was at [[36, 17], [36, 18], [37, 18], [37, 17]] (center 36.5, 17.5) and moved to [[34, 18], [34, 19], [35, 19], [35, 18]] (center 34.5, 18.5). So it moved up 2 and right 1.
I clicked at (35, 19). The block's old center was (36.5, 17.5). The click was at (35, 19), which is up 1.5 and right 1.5 from the center. The block moved up 2 and right 1.
So the block moves toward the clicked position. It seems to move by about 2 cells in the direction of the click. Let me verify this by trying another move.
Now I need to figure out where the blocks should go. The blue blob is at (27, 33). Maybe I need to move all magenta blocks to converge on the blue blob?
Current magenta positions:
- (34, 18) - need to move up and right toward (27, 33)
- (40, 36) - need to move up and left toward (27, 33)
- (56, 46) - need to move up and left toward (27, 33)
- (56, 16) - need to move up and right toward (27, 33)
Let me try to move the blocks toward the blue blob. I'll click on positions that guide each block toward the center.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>

# Current positions:

# For (56, 46), click above and to the left: (54, 44)
# For (56, 16), click above and to the right: (54, 18)

# Let me test with one block first
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 32, 'col': 20}])
</parameter>
